title: A New Antibiotic Class Was Found in Backyard Soil. The Pipeline to Patients Barely Exists.
description: Scientists discovered lariocidin — a structurally knotted molecule from a Hamilton, Ontario backyard — that kills drug-resistant superbugs through a mechanism no antibiotic has ever used before. In mice infected with one of the WHO's most dangerous pathogens, it achieved 100% survival. The harder question is whether a broken pharmaceutical economy will let it save a single human life.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for A Backyard Soil Sample Just Rewrote the Antibiotic Playbook. Getting It to Patients Is Another Problem Entirely.
